text.title: python-fastapi Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for python-fastapi as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Defining def route(...) instead of async def in a FastAPI router.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sync route handlers block the event loop for the entire duration of the handler, preventing concurrent request processing.

failure-mode.description: Calling sync ORM code (session.query(...)) inside async def routes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sync ORM calls block the event loop thread. Use SQLAlchemy 2 async API (await db.execute(select(...))).

failure-mode.description: Using Pydantic v1 idioms: class Config, @validator, .dict(), orm_mode=True.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: v1 idioms compile and run in v2 with wrong semantics — validators may not fire, orm_mode is renamed. Pin pydantic>=2 and ban v1 patterns explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Using FastAPI BackgroundTasks for jobs longer than ~100ms (email delivery, report generation, video processing).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: BackgroundTasks runs after the response in the same event loop. Long tasks delay the loop and exhaust worker capacity for new requests. Use Celery/Arq/Taskiq.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without response_model, FastAPI serializes all model fields including server-only fields (hashed_password, internal IDs). Also breaks OpenAPI documentation.

failure-mode.description: Setting expire_on_commit=False globally then using the same session in a long-lived background worker.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: expire_on_commit=False is correct for request-scoped sessions (prevents lazy-loading after commit). In long-lived workers it serves stale data without warning.
